[[BLOCK:slide53_shape0]]
•   "Individuals with type 2 diabetes, regardless of blood sugar control, often experience elevated Triglycerides (TCH), reduced High-Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol (HDL), and sometimes increased Low-Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol (LDL)... The American Diabetes Association recommends checking cholesterol levels at least once a year. If cholesterol levels are high and not controlled by medication, more frequent monitoring is advised. For individuals with diabetes but without known coronary heart disease, it is recommended that blood LDL levels be below 100 milligrams per deciliter (mg/dL), HDL levels above 50 mg/dL, and Triglycerides below 150 mg/dL."
•   Source: https://www.verywellhealth.com/the-dangers-of-high-cholesterol-and-diabetes-3571713
